title: Summed heat second differences telescope to boundary gradients

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The sum over a finite range of the heat second-difference operator equals the left boundary gradient plus the right boundary gradient.
assumptions: u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n is a natural-number range length
informal.expected_conclusion: The finite sum of heatSecondDifference telescopes to heatBoundaryTerm.

declarations.name: heatSecondDifference
declarations.statement: def heatSecondDifference (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := u i - 2 * u (i + 1) + u (i + 2)

declarations.name: heatBoundaryTerm
declarations.statement: def heatBoundaryTerm (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) : Real := u 0 - u 1 + (u (n + 1) - u n)

declarations.name: heatSecondDifference_sum_range
declarations.statement: theorem heatSecondDifference_sum_range (u : Nat -> Real) : forall n : Nat, Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => heatSecondDifference u i) = heatBoundaryTerm u n

tasks.input: Translate this explicit heat-equation total-balance theorem into Lean 4: The sum over a finite range of the heat second-difference operator equals the left boundary gradient plus the right boundary gradient.
tasks.target: theorem heatSecondDifference_sum_range (u : Nat -> Real) : forall n : Nat, Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => heatSecondDifference u i) = heatBoundaryTerm u n

tasks.input: Given the heat-equation scaffold definitions and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by induction on n, using Finset.sum_range_succ, unfolding definitions, and ring.

tasks.input: Find the Mathlib theorem used to peel off the last term of a range sum.
